title: Marketplace Matching Indie Founders With Launch Reviewers

input.idea: Marketplace matching indie founders with launch reviewers.
input.target_user: Indie founders preparing launches and reviewers who can give useful launch feedback.
input.use_case: Founders would use it before launch to get credible feedback on positioning, page, offer, and launch plan.
input.user_context: User requested a marketplace/platform Persona run.

evidence_packet.claim: Product Hunt provides launch checklists and says makers should ask people to visit and comment, not directly upvote.
evidence_packet.relevance: Launch preparation has clear demand, but platform rules shape acceptable reviewer behavior.

evidence_packet.claim: Product Hunt is built around product listings, comments, votes, and discovery by makers and tech users.
evidence_packet.relevance: Feedback and visibility already happen in-market, reducing the need for a separate marketplace.

evidence_packet.claim: Expert explanations of SaaS and creator landing pages already exist as curated content.
evidence_packet.relevance: Review supply can be packaged as content or consulting without a marketplace.

evidence_packet.claim: Product Hunt became influential because makers, journalists, investors, and creators gather around launches.
evidence_packet.relevance: The strongest value may be network access, not reviewer matching alone.

verdict_metrics.name: likely pull
verdict_metrics.reason: Founders want feedback, but marketplaces need trust and liquidity

verdict_metrics.name: urgency
verdict_metrics.reason: Launch deadlines create a short buying window

verdict_metrics.name: willingness to pay
verdict_metrics.reason: Payment depends on reviewer credibility and output quality

verdict_metrics.name: trust barrier
verdict_metrics.reason: Bad reviewers waste scarce launch time

verdict_metrics.name: differentiation
verdict_metrics.reason: Communities, consultants, and Product Hunt already absorb the job

hard_nos: If reviewer quality is uneven, founders will not risk launch timing on strangers.
If supply is unpaid or low-paid, good reviewers will churn or route buyers off-platform.
If reviews do not produce visible launch improvements, founders will treat it as nice-to-have feedback.
If the marketplace promises exposure, it may conflict with Product Hunt norms around direct upvote asks.
objections_to_beat: Manually match 10 founders with 5 vetted reviewers and measure whether reviews change launch assets.
Define reviewer quality with public examples, launch experience, and founder ratings before opening supply.
Start with one packaged service, such as 48-hour landing-page plus launch-comment review, not an open marketplace.
Test whether founders pay for reviewer credibility, turnaround time, or distribution access.

next_experiment.action: Concierge-match 8 founders launching within 45 days with 4 handpicked reviewers; charge founders $49 for a 48-hour written review and pay reviewers $25.
next_experiment.why: This tests both sides of liquidity, reviewer quality, and unit economics before building marketplace software.
next_experiment.success_signal: At least 5 founders pay, 4 submit revised assets after review, and 3 reviewers accept a second paid assignment.

personas.label: Urgent launch buyer
personas.objection: They need fast feedback but fear wasting the launch window.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A vetted reviewer with examples and guaranteed 48-hour turnaround.

personas.label: Opportunistic reviewer
personas.objection: They may join for leads, then move clients off-platform.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Enough paid volume and clear rules around off-platform work.

personas.label: Trust-sensitive founder
personas.objection: A stranger review feels lower-trust than asking known founders.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Reviewer proof from people with relevant launch wins.

personas.label: Repeat indie launcher
personas.objection: They launch often and value faster critique loops.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A saved bench of reviewers who learn their product style.

personas.label: Supply skeptic
personas.objection: Good reviewers have better-paid consulting or audience channels.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A narrow paid package that makes reviewer time worth it.

personas.label: Niche expert reviewer
personas.objection: They only want matches in their niche.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Category-specific matching and higher-priced expert reviews.

personas.label: Marketplace power user
personas.objection: They already buy launch help and compare providers.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A fast queue, transparent ratings, and reusable reviewer relationships.

personas.label: Cold-start casualty
personas.objection: Without dense supply and demand, the marketplace is empty at the wrong time.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Concierge matching that hides liquidity until demand repeats.

personas.label: Price-shopping founder
personas.objection: They will compare against free communities and cheap consultants.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A low-cost fixed deliverable with better examples than free feedback.

personas.label: High-risk launch
personas.objection: They need confidentiality and domain knowledge.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: NDA-ready reviewers with category expertise and private workflow.

personas.label: Community insider
personas.objection: They already have founder friends and group chats.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Access to reviewers outside their current network with proven launch taste.

personas.label: One-time launcher
personas.objection: They may pay once, but retention is weak.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A one-shot package priced for acquisition, not long-term marketplace usage.
